title: Mateo's Brave Night Light
synopsis: When Mateo's night light breaks, he feels scared of the dark bedroom. With help from his mama and papa, he discovers he can be brave and finds comfort in the gentle moonlight, learning that being scared is okay and that he has the courage to face his fears.

pages.text: Mateo loved his little blue night light. It made soft, happy light in his bedroom every night. But tonight, when Mama turned it on, nothing happened. The night light was broken!

illustration.description: A cozy bedroom with warm yellow walls and a small bed with colorful blankets. Mateo, a 3-year-old Latino boy with curly dark hair and brown eyes, stands next to his mama, a woman with long dark hair wearing a soft pink sweater. They're both looking at a small blue night light plugged into the wall that isn't glowing. Mateo's expression shows disappointment and concern.
illustration.alt: Mateo and his mama looking at a broken night light in his bedroom

pages.text: Mateo felt scared. His room looked so dark without the friendly blue glow. "I don't like the dark," he said, holding Mama's hand tight. His bottom lip started to wobble.

illustration.description: Close-up of Mateo's face showing worried, scared expression with wide brown eyes and a trembling lip. The room behind him is dimmer now, with soft shadows. His mama's hand is gently holding his small hand. The lighting is warm but muted, creating a slightly anxious mood with deep blue and purple shadows.
illustration.alt: Mateo looking scared in his dimmer bedroom, holding his mama's hand

pages.text: "It's okay to feel scared," Mama said gently. "Sometimes I feel scared too. But you know what? You are very brave, Mateo. Let's ask Papa to help us find another way to make you feel safe."

illustration.description: Mama kneeling down to Mateo's eye level, her face kind and reassuring with a gentle smile. She has her arms around Mateo in a comforting hug. The room is softly lit by hallway light streaming through the doorway. Both characters have warm, loving expressions, and the overall mood is nurturing and safe.
illustration.alt: Mama comforting Mateo with a gentle hug in his bedroom

pages.text: Papa came into the room. He was tall with kind eyes and a warm smile. "What if we open the curtains?" he suggested. "Look, Mateo! The moon is shining tonight, just like a big night light in the sky."

illustration.description: Papa, a Latino man with short black hair and a beard, wearing a blue plaid shirt, is pulling back soft green curtains at the window. Bright moonlight streams into the room, creating a magical silver glow. Mateo and Mama watch from beside the bed, their faces lit up with wonder and hope.
illustration.alt: Papa opening curtains to reveal moonlight streaming into Mateo's bedroom

pages.text: The moonlight made everything look magical and safe. Mateo felt his scared feelings getting smaller. "The moon is like a friend watching over me," he said, feeling brave and happy now.

illustration.description: The bedroom now glows with soft, silvery moonlight coming through the open window. Mateo stands confidently in the center, arms slightly outstretched, with a big smile on his face. His parents stand nearby, smiling proudly. The room looks peaceful and magical, with the moon visible through the window casting gentle shadows that look friendly rather than scary.
illustration.alt: Mateo standing happily in his moonlit bedroom with his proud parents

pages.text: Mateo climbed into bed, feeling cozy and safe. "Good night, Moon," he whispered. "Thank you for being my night light." He closed his eyes, no longer scared, but brave and ready for sweet dreams.

illustration.description: Mateo tucked snugly in his bed under colorful blankets, with a peaceful, sleepy expression on his face. The moonlight creates a gentle glow across his pillow. His parents give him goodnight kisses, and the whole scene radiates warmth, love, and tranquility. The moon is visible through the window like a gentle guardian.
illustration.alt: Mateo peacefully tucked in bed with moonlight shining through his window

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Mateo feel when his night light broke?
questions.explanation: Mateo felt scared when his night light broke because his room looked dark without it.

questions.question: Who helped Mateo solve his problem?
questions.explanation: Mama and Papa helped Mateo by opening the curtains to let the moonlight in.

questions.question: What did Papa suggest to make Mateo feel better?
questions.explanation: Papa suggested opening the curtains so the moonlight could shine in like a night light.

questions.question: How did Mateo feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: At the end, Mateo felt brave and happy because the moonlight made him feel safe.

pages.title: Complete Mateo's Story
pages.text: Mateo's night light was _____. He felt _____ of the dark. But the _____ made him feel safe again.

pages.title: Mateo's Feelings
pages.text: When Mateo felt scared, his _____ helped him. They opened the _____ to let in moonlight. Now Mateo feels _____.
